definition: Private credit ($1.7T+) with appraisal-based NAV returns (autocorrelation 0.5-0.7) constitutes a hidden vol reservoir where true economic vol is 2-3x reported. No traded vol surface captures this risk. Semi-liquid vehicles ($300B+ in interval funds, non-traded BDCs, perpetual structures) offering quarterly 5% NAV redemptions against illiquid portfolios present a liquidity mismatch vulnerability structurally identical to UK open-ended property funds (2022).
evidence: Appraisal-based NAV returns exhibit autocorrelation of 0.5-0.7
Unsmoothing reveals economic vol is 2-3x reported
Semi-liquid vehicles offer quarterly 5% NAV redemptions against illiquid portfolios
Structurally identical to UK open-ended property funds (2022)
Identified as most important novel contribution sitting outside traditional vol surface analysis
Correctly framed as measurement gap not mispricing
open_questions: Are manager liquidity sleeves (20-30% in liquid assets) sufficient?
What trigger duration of sustained high-vol credit regime activates the vulnerability?
What is the aggregate liquidity mismatch — not precisely tracked?
Could private credit opacity disrupt standard vol transmission sequencing?
